methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 15 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Anduril Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (SPAC), post-money $78B+

data.narrative:
True Anomaly, a defense and security-focused frontier-tech company founded by Even Rogers, completed a SPAC transaction at a $2.2B valuation after raising $600M to date. The peer universe for valuation consists of 15 same-sector and similar-valuation-tier comparable companies, spanning 2 public and 8 private firms. Top private peers include Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI, with public comparables trading under DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

A peer-median triangulation method was applied across the comparable set. The 15-firm cohort exhibits a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M at the floor to $9.2B at the ceiling. Sample peers within this distribution include Saronic at $9.2B (Series D), Flock Safety at $7.5B (Series E), Shield AI at $5.6B (Series G), Chaos Industries at $4.5B (Series D), and Skydio at $2.2B (Series E).

The analysis supports a triangulated valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, with a median of $1.5B. True Anomaly's current $2.2B SPAC valuation sits 47% above the peer median, implying either a premium justified by differentiated defensibility or execution roadmap, or overvaluation relative to this peer set. Key uncertainties include revenue and growth trajectory, competitive positioning relative to the identified peer cohort, and execution risk on SPAC-era commitments.
